பாடக்குறிப்புகள்.விவரங்கள்: Students learn to identify various objects found in a play area. Sky and Nature: Sky, clouds, Sun, tree. Play Equipment: Swing, slide, bench. Toys and Games: Kite, bat, ball, top, balloon, bicycle, marbles. Others: Tank, van, gate, dog.
This song introduces several action words to encourage physical activity. Actions: Clap, stamp, skip, run, turn around, hop, jump, bend, and stretch. Theme: Playing together and having fun.

This comic strip highlights the value of inclusion through a small rat. Characters and Games: Dogs: Playing cricket. Rabbit: Flying a kite. Monkeys: Swinging on a tree. Frogs: Playing hopscotch. Conflict: The rat wants to join every game but is refused (e.g., 'you are late,' 'only one kite,' 'rats cannot swing'). Resolution: The rat builds a sandcastle. The other animals see it and ask to join. The rat invites everyone, concluding that 'Playing together is always fun'.

This section introduces letters and animals along with their actions: J - Jaguar. K - Kangaroo: Hops along the path. L - Lion: Sleeping in the grass. M - Monkey: Climbs a tree. N - Nightingale. O - Owl: Looks at the monkey. P - Parrot: Dances on a branch. Q - Quail. R - Rabbit: Runs into a hole.
Sounds: j (jug), k (kite), l (lion), m (monkey), n (net), o (orange), p (pen), q (queen), r (rat). Grammar: Using the structure 'has' (e.g., 'He has a kite,' 'She has a bat').
A story focused on colors and the moral 'Sharing is caring'. Characters and Colors Shared: Dolphin: Gets blue. Crab: Gets red. Seahorse: Gets yellow. Star fish: Gets green.
Students learn to use social etiquette words. Thank you: Used when receiving a gift. Sorry: Used when a mistake is made (like breaking a glass).

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: What is the title of the second unit in the English textbook?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The second unit focuses on the theme of play and is titled 'Play Time'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: Name three playthings mentioned in the 'Look and say' section.
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The vocabulary section introduces toys and games like kite, bat, ball, top, balloon, bicycle, and marbles.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: In the song 'Clap your hands,' what action follows 'Stamp your feet'?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The song introduces action words in sequence, such as clap, stamp, skip, and run.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: Which animal is trying to join the games in the story 'Come, Let us Play'?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The story highlights a small rat who wants to play but is initially refused by the other animals.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: What game are the dogs playing?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: In the comic strip 'Come, Let us Play', the dogs are playing cricket.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: Why can't the rat fly a kite with the rabbit?
ஆப்ஷன்கள்: The rabbit has only one kite
The rat does not like kites
கேள்விகள்.விடை: The rabbit has only one kite
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The rabbit refuses to let the rat join because there is 'only one kite'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: What does the rat build at the end of the story?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The rat builds a sandcastle, which attracts the other animals to join.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: Complete the sentence from the story: 'Playing together is always ________.'
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The moral of the story concludes that inclusion and 'Playing together is always fun'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: In the Alphabet Jungle, which animal is sleeping in the grass?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: According to the Alphabet Jungle, 'L - Lion: Sleeping in the grass'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: What does the Kangaroo do in the Alphabet Jungle?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: In the Alphabet Jungle, 'K - Kangaroo: Hops along the path'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: Which bird dances on a branch in the Alphabet Jungle?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The text states 'P - Parrot: Dances on a branch'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: Where does the little Rabbit run into?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: In the Alphabet Jungle, 'R - Rabbit: Runs into a hole'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: Give the letter and the example word for 'O' in the Phonics section.
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The phonics section teaches the sound for 'o' using the word 'orange'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: What word is used to show possession in the 'Let us talk' section (e.g., 'Kavin ________ a ball')?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The grammar section introduces the structure 'has' (e.g., 'He has a kite,' 'She has a bat').

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: In 'The Magic Fish,' what color were the sea animals before getting colors? (Implied)
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: Stories like 'The Magic Fish' typically start with the animals lacking color (plain or white) before the magic fish shares its colors.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: What color does the Crab receive from the Magic Fish?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: According to the story details, the 'Crab: Gets red'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: Which animal receives the color yellow?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: In the story, the 'Seahorse: Gets yellow'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: What is the main message/moral of 'The Magic Fish' story?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The story is focused on sharing colors and teaches the moral 'Sharing is caring'.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: If you break a glass, what magic word should you use?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The word 'Sorry' is used as social etiquette when a mistake is made, like breaking a glass.

கேள்விகள்.கேள்வி: What is the starting and ending letter of the word 'marbles'?
கேள்விகள்.விளக்கம்: The word 'marbles' starts with the letter 'm' and ends with the letter 's'.
